implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-003: missing manifest degrades to empty flags + single warning
AC-004: always_include prepended regardless of filter
AC-005: depth-2 cap with context_manifest_cycle_detected warning
AC-007: read_allowlist exclusion with warning naming the path
AC-008: repo-root escape rejected with warning
AC-009: stateless, independent ResolvedContext per call
AC-010: stable ordering — always_include, categories, declaration
AC-011: symlinks resolved before allowlist check
AC-012: Graphiti-bypass via KeyError + exhaustive unit test coverage
requirements_remaining: AC-013: explicit lint/format gate run (pending CI runner identification)

completion_promises.criterion_text: resolve_context_flags() in src/forge/adapters/guardkit/context_resolver.py returns a ResolvedContext with flags, paths, warnings
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented `resolve_context_flags()` returning a frozen `ResolvedContext` dataclass with `flags: list[str]`, `paths: list[str]`, and `warnings: list[GuardKitWarning]` fields. Verified by TestResolvedContextShape::test_resolved_context_has_three_named_attributes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER matches DDR-005 verbatim (9 entries — Graphiti subcommands are intentionally absent)
completion_promises.evidence: _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER has exactly 9 keys (system-arch, system-design, system-plan, feature-spec, feature-plan, task-review, task-work, task-complete, autobuild) with sets matching DDR-005 verbatim. No graphiti-* keys. Verified by TestCommandCategoryFilterMatchesDDR005 (4 tests including parametrised graphiti-absence checks).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Missing manifest -> returns empty flags, single GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_missing', ...), never raises
completion_promises.evidence: When `.guardkit/context-manifest.yaml` is absent, `resolve_context_flags` returns `ResolvedContext(flags=[], paths=[], warnings=[GuardKitWarning(code='context_manifest_missing', ...)])` with no exception. Verified by TestMissingManifestDegradesGracefully (3 tests: empty flags, single warning with correct code, never raises).

completion_promises.criterion_text: internal_docs.always_include paths are prepended to the flag list regardless of category filter
completion_promises.evidence: always_include paths are read from origin manifest and prepended to `paths`/`flags` before any category-filtered docs. Even an uncategorised doc is included regardless of the subcommand's filter. Verified by TestAlwaysIncludePrepended (3 tests: ordering, uncategorised doc, flags pairing).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Dependency chase follows manifests up to depth 2 then stops with context_manifest_cycle_detected warning
completion_promises.evidence: BFS over manifests with `_DEPTH_CAP = 2`. At depth+1 > _DEPTH_CAP a warning with code `context_manifest_cycle_detected` and `details.reason='depth_cap'` is emitted. Verified by TestDepthCapChase (3 tests: depth-1 chase, depth-2 chase, depth-3 stop with warning).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cycle detection: a manifest already visited in the current chain is not re-visited
completion_promises.evidence: Per-chain visited set (frozenset) carried with each BFS frame. When a dep manifest is already in the chain, recursion is skipped and a `context_manifest_cycle_detected` warning with `details.reason='cycle'` is emitted. Verified by TestCycleDetection::test_two_node_cycle_resolves_safely.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Documents whose resolved absolute path is outside read_allowlist are omitted with a structured warning naming the omitted path
completion_promises.evidence: `_validate_doc_path()` checks `_is_within(resolved, allowed)` for each entry of read_allowlist. If none match, the doc is omitted and a `context_manifest_path_outside_allowlist` warning is emitted with `details.path` set to the omitted absolute path. Verified by TestReadAllowlistExclusion::test_doc_outside_allowlist_is_omitted_and_warned.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Documents whose path resolves outside the repo root are omitted with a structured warning
completion_promises.evidence: `_validate_doc_path()` first checks `_is_within(resolved, owning_repo_root)`. If the resolved path escapes the repo root (e.g. via `..`), a `context_manifest_path_outside_repo` warning is emitted and the doc is omitted. Verified by TestDocPathEscapesRepoRoot::test_path_with_dotdot_escape_is_rejected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Resolution is stateless — two concurrent calls against the same repo_path produce independent ResolvedContext values, no module-level cache
completion_promises.evidence: No module-level cache (no `@functools.cache`, no module-level mutable state). Each call constructs fresh lists for `flags`, `paths`, `warnings`. Verified by TestStatelessness::test_two_calls_return_independent_lists which mutates the first result and asserts the second is unaffected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Order is stable: always_include first, then categories in _COMMAND_CATEGORY_FILTER insertion order, then by manifest declaration order within each category
completion_promises.evidence: always_include paths prepended; then docs bucketed per-category and emitted in canonical `_CATEGORY_ORDER` (architecture, decisions, specs, contracts, source — first-appearance order in DDR-005 dict values). Within each category bucket, docs are appended in BFS+declaration order. Verified by TestStableOrder::test_always_include_then_categories_then_declaration_order with mixed-category fixtures.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Symlinks are followed before allowlist check
completion_promises.evidence: `_validate_doc_path()` calls `candidate.resolve(strict=False)` before either the repo-root or allowlist checks. `resolve()` follows symlinks, so a symlink pointing outside the repo or allowlist is correctly rejected. Verified by TestSymlinksFollowedBeforeAllowlistCheck::test_symlink_target_outside_allowlist_is_rejected (skipped on Windows).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Exhaustive unit tests covering: missing manifest, depth-1 chase, depth-2 chase, depth-3 stop, two-node cycle, allowlist-omitted doc, escaping-path doc, Graphiti-bypass-by-caller (resolver raises KeyError on Graphiti subcommand keys)
completion_promises.evidence: 28 tests across 11 test classes mirror each acceptance criterion. Graphiti scenario verified by TestGraphitiSubcommandsRaiseKeyError (parametrised over graphiti-search/export/import/prune + unknown subcommand) which asserts `KeyError`. All other listed scenarios have dedicated test methods. All 28 tests pass; full guardkit suite (124 tests) also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Files follow PEP 8, are typed (`from __future__ import annotations`), use Black-compatible 88-char line length, and pass pytest cleanly. Project-configured lint commands (e.g. ruff/black) were not invoked because no lint runner config was located in pyproject.toml; running pytest succeeded with no warnings from the implementation/test files. If a CI lint stage exists it should be re-checked.
